title: Talk:Flour Massacre
wikitext:
{{Talk header}} {{WikiProject banner shell|class=B|1= {{WikiProject Crime and Criminal Biography|terrorism=yes|terrorism-imp=Low|importance=Low}} {{WikiProject Death|importance=Low}} {{WikiProject Human rights|importance=High}} {{WikiProject Israel|importance=Low}} {{WikiProject Israel Palestine Collaboration}} {{WikiProject Palestine|importance=Mid}} {{WikiProject Military history|class=C|Middle-Eastern=yes|Post-Cold-War=yes|b1=no|b2=yes|b3=yes|b4=yes|b5=no|list=no}} {{WikiProject Disaster management|importance= Low}} {{WikiProject Firearms |importance=Low}} }} {{ArbCom Arab-Israeli enforcement}} {{old move|date1=29 February 2024|destination1=Al-Rashid massacre|result1=Moved to [[Flour massacre]]|link1=Special:Permalink/1212513347#Requested move 29 February 2024|date2=28 April 2024|destination2=Al-Rashid killings|result2=Withdrawn|link2=Special:Permalink/1221418875#Requested move 28 April 2024|date3=14 April 2025|from3=Flour massacre|destination3=Flour Massacre|result3=moved|link3=Special:Permalink/1286113515#Requested move 14 April 2025}} == On leaving out the bits we don't like == The incident occurred when the crowd blocked and started plundering an aid convoy that was on its way to another part of Gaza City. This fact, which I have never seen disputed, is totally suppressed, presumably because editors fear it might mitigate the brutality of the Israelis (it doesn't). I've corrected the most blatant attempt in the introduction to make it sound like a planned aid distribution, but this fiction is sustained throughout the article, by both commission and omission. [[User:Utilisateur19911|Utilisateur19911]] ([[User talk:Utilisateur19911|talk]]) 09:48, 8 April 2024 (UTC) :This seems like quite a good example of how not to write things in the topic area. Congrats. Unfortunately, information about mental states can't be reliably extracted from edits. If the article had been written by an AI but you didn't know, you thought it was written by a bunch of devious rascals, you probably would have made exactly the same revisions for the same policy-based reasons. So, it's about the content. [[User:Sean.hoyland|Sean.hoyland]] ([[User talk:Sean.hoyland|talk]]) 12:19, 8 April 2024 (UTC) ::"So, it's about the content." — which you do not address. [[User:Utilisateur19911|Utilisateur19911]] ([[User talk:Utilisateur19911|talk]]) 07:33, 12 April 2024 (UTC) == Not a planned aid distribution == There is no mention that the crowd blocked and plundered an aid convoy that was on its way to another part of Gaza City. There is euphemistic and ambiguous language that gives the impression it was a planned aid distribution. [[User:Utilisateur19911|Utilisateur19911]] ([[User talk:Utilisateur19911|talk]]) 10:13, 14 April 2024 (UTC) :Has nothing to do with the massacre. The aid distribution was indeed planned between Palestinian businessmen and Israeli authorities. The only ambiguity here is this comment here. [[User:Makeandtoss|Makeandtoss]] ([[User talk:Makeandtoss|talk]]) 12:12, 6 May 2024 (UTC) This lasted just under two weeks on the page (see edit history for sources): {{citation bloc|Rumours had circulated that an aid convoy would be passing along Al-Rashid Street,[12][13][14][15][16] and "thousands"[17][18] of people had gathered there before dawn to intercept it.[19][20] The crowd "descended on the line of vehicles as it travelled in darkness northwards along the coastal road".[21] The convoy — an Israeli initiative using trucks supplied by Palestinian businessmen[22] — "was moving towards the city centre"[23] on its way to a "destination in northern Gaza".[24]}} {{citation bloc|The plunder of aid convoys was a problem in Gaza during the Israel–Hamas war.[25][26][27] ''The New York Times'' reported that "U.N. aid convoys carrying essential goods to northern Gaza have been looted, either by civilians fearing starvation or by organized gangs",[28] while an Egyptian convoy driver said that "the trucks were often looted by Gazans because of the scarcity of aid in the enclave".[29] On 20 February, a few days before the Al-Rashid Street incident, U.N. aid agencies announced that "food deliveries to war-torn northern Gaza had been suspended after looting attacks".[30] Israel responded by organising a series of convoys itself, including the one on 29 February.[22]}} [[User:Utilisateur19911|Utilisateur19911]] ([[User talk:Utilisateur19911|talk]]) 19:29, 7 May 2024 (UTC) == Requested move 28 April 2024 ==
:''The following is a closed discussion of a [[Wikipedia:Requested moves|requested move]]. '''Please do not modify it.''' Subsequent comments should be made in a new section on the talk page. Editors desiring to contest the closing decision should consider a [[Wikipedia:move review|move review]] after discussing it on the closer's talk page. No further edits should be made to this discussion.'' The result of the move request was: '''Withdrawn.''' Per request from an admin, will withdraw for now to be revisited in three to six months ([[Wikipedia:Requested moves/Closing instructions#Closure by a page mover|closed by non-admin page mover]]) [[User:BilledMammal|BilledMammal]] ([[User talk:BilledMammal|talk]]) 21:50, 29 April 2024 (UTC) ---- [[:Flour massacre]] → {{no redirect|Al-Rashid killings}} – Per [[WP:NCENPOV]], we should only call an event a massacre if "massacre" is part of the [[WP:COMMONNAME]], or if it is a {{tq|generally accepted word used when identifying the event}}. There is no common name for this event, and sources generally do not [[Use-mention distinction|use]] "massacre" when referring to it. For example, working down the list of generally reliable sources at [[WP:RSP]]: #[[ABC News]] - Does not use "massacre" ([https://abcnews.go.com/International/live-updates/israel-gaza-hamas-war/us-dropped-aid-into-gaza-two-officials-confirm-107742228?id=107535302]) #[[The Age]] - Does not use "massacre" ([https://www.theage.com.au/world/middle-east/israeli-troops-fire-on-palestinians-seeking-food-israel-says-deadly-scene-was-a-chaotic-stampede-20240301-p5f8zs.html]) #[[Al Jazeera]] - Uses "massacre" ([https://www.aljazeera.com/news/longform/2024/3/5/the-blood-was-everywhere-inside-israels-flour-massacre-in-gaza]) #[[Associated Press]] - Does not use "massacre" ([https://apnews.com/article/biden-meloni-white-house-ukraine-112dacd932a45f42621a25b304ae5d99]) #[[The Australian]] - Does not use "massacre" ([https://www.theaustralian.com.au/world/israeli-forces-fire-on-crowds-surrounding-gaza-aid-trucks/news-story/]) #[[Axios]] - Does not use "massacre" ([https://www.axios.com/2024/02/29/gaza-aid-palestinians-killed-israel-idf-hamas]) #[[BBC]] - Does not use "massacre" ([https://www.bbc.com/news/world-middle-east-68443883]) #[[Bloomberg]] - Does not use "massacre" ([https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2024-03-07/hopes-fade-of-gaza-cease-fire-before-ramadan-after-failed-talks]) #[[CNN]] - Does not use "massacre" ([https://edition.cnn.com/2024/04/09/middleeast/gaza-food-aid-convoy-deaths-eyewitness-intl-investigation-cmd/index.html]) #[[The Daily Telegraph]] - Does not use "massacre" ([https://www.telegraph.co.uk/world-news/2024/03/02/us-troops-airdrop-aid-gaza/]) #[[Deutsche Welle]] - Does not use "massacre" ([https://www.dw.com/en/israel-hamas-war-us-plans-to-airdrop-aid-into-gaza-strip/live-68412583#liveblog-post-68418049]) #[[Financial Times]] - Does not use "massacre" ([https://www.ft.com/content/cff17860-86fa-416b-85b7-2861ea83249e]) #[[Forbes]] - Does not use "massacre" ([https://www.forbes.com/sites/tylerroush/2024/02/29/more-than-100-palestinians-killed-while-waiting-for-food-in-gaza-health-ministry-says/?sh=75a2bdd95d54]) #[[The Globe and Mail]] - Does not use "massacre" ([https://www.theglobeandmail.com/world/article-biden-approves-military-airdrops-of-aid-into-gaza-after-chaotic/]) #[[The Guardian]] - Does not use "massacre" ([https://www.theguardian.com/world/2024/feb/29/gaza-aid-trucks-death-toll-explainer]) #[[Haaretz]] - Does not use "massacre" ([https://www.haaretz.com/israel-news/2024-04-03/ty-article/.premium/even-before-israel-killed-aid-workers-in-gaza-the-world-was-running-out-of-patience/0000018e-a06e-d4f2-a9cf-b87e0a960000]) #[[The Hill (newspaper)]] - Does not use "massacre" ([https://thehill.com/homenews/ap/ap-international/ap-strike-on-palestinians-waiting-for-aid-in-gaza-kills-and-wounds-dozens/]) #[[The Hindu]] - Does not use "massacre" ([https://www.thehindu.com/news/international/calls-for-probe-ceasefire-follow-israeli-gunfire-near-aid-convoy/article67904572.ece]) #[[The Independent]] - Does not use "massacre" ([https://www.independent.co.uk/news/world/middle-east/israel-gaza-deaths-aid-un-b2505515.html]) #[[The Jewish Chronicle]] - Does not use "massacre" ([https://www.thejc.com/news/uk/bbc-verify-cites-journalist-who-mourned-death-of-terrorist-sqn61ky6]) #[[Jacobin]] - Uses "massacre" ([https://jacobin.com/2024/04/israel-gaza-war-crimes-genocide-idf]) #[[Los Angeles Times]] - Does not use "massacre" ([https://www.latimes.com/world-nation/story/2024-04-05/israel-routinely-promises-to-investigate-killings-of-civilians-with-few-results]) As such, our use of it is against policy, and now that we can look at this event as one in the past, rather than one in the present, I think it is time to revisit what we should title the article. [[User:BilledMammal|BilledMammal]] ([[User talk:BilledMammal|talk]]) 18:40, 28 April 2024 (UTC) :'''Oppose''' - The proposed title is too euphemistic and generic. Even before the current change the article was called something like '''Al Rashid humanitarian aid killings'''. The title '''Flour massacre''' also differentiates this incident from subsequent humanitarian aid killings and conveys the notability of the incident, its significant death toll, and the reactions to it, all significant parts of the article. Many of the sources which do not use "massacre" were written shortly after the event, so I do not find the argument that sources are moving away from calling it a massacre to be a credible or persuasive one. [[User:Unbandito|Unbandito]] ([[User talk:Unbandito|talk]]) 00:36, 29 April 2024 (UTC) ::Previously, the article was at '''Al Rashid humanitarian aid incident'''. Further, the argument isn’t that sources are moving away; it’s that they’ve never used massacre, and they continue to not use massacre - per our PAG’s, this means we can not use massacre. [[User:BilledMammal|BilledMammal]] ([[User talk:BilledMammal|talk]]) 00:40, 29 April 2024 (UTC) *'''Oppose''' several sources flat out say this is known as the Flour Massacre, it is the common name and the attempt at repeating a failed proposal because you did not like the result or the result of the move review is in bad faith. Should be closed and absent sources actually disputing that this is known as the Flour massacre there should be a moratorium put in place to prevent further bad faith efforts to overturn a well established consensus. '''[[User talk:Nableezy|nableezy]]''' - 07:50, 29 April 2024 (UTC) *'''Strong oppose''': Flour Massacre is a common name as evidenced by its mention in CNN, Al Jazeera, Middle East Eye, Washington Post, Haaretz, Jacobin, and France24 and by several UN officials.[[User:Makeandtoss|Makeandtoss]] ([[User talk:Makeandtoss|talk]]) 11:18, 29 April 2024 (UTC) *'''Strong oppose''' Needs to be closed immediately as an abuse of process. This was taken to move review last month where the previous RM was endorsed. The nom participated in that move review. This is simply a blatant refusal to accept a community consensus and a desire to bludgeon the community into accepting their desired course of action through sheer exhaustion. There is absolutely no justification for another RM so soon. This is absolutely unacceptable behaviour. [[User:AusLondonder|AusLondonder]] ([[User talk:AusLondonder|talk]]) 17:01, 29 April 2024 (UTC) :'''Oppose + procedural close:''' No new information has been been presented since the previous endorsed close. On the contrary, the opening statement appears to be a copy-paste of the same evidence presented in the previous RM in a comment on 3 March. Simply pestering the community with identikit arguments is clear abuse of process, and if nothing has changed it means the RM should never have been opened. [[User:Iskandar323|Iskandar323]] ([[User talk:Iskandar323|talk]]) 19:53, 29 April 2024 (UTC) ===Survey=== ===Discussion=== :Don't see what changed since the previous RM, which was endorsed at move review, where you wrote: :Working through WP:RSP, it is clear that there is not a consensus in reliable sources: :ABC News - Does not use "massacre" :The Age - Does not use "massacre" :Al Jazeera - Uses "massacre" :Associated Press - Does not use "massacre" :The Australian - Does not use "massacre" :Axios - Does not use "massacre" :BBC - Does not use "massacre" :Bloomberg - Does not use "massacre" :CNN - Does not use "massacre" :The Daily Telegraph - Does not use "massacre" :Deutsche Welle - Does not use "massacre" :Financial Times - Does not use "massacre" :Forbes - Does not use "massacre" :The Globe and Mail - Does not use "massacre" :The Guardian - Does not use "massacre" :Haaretz - Does not use "massacre" :The Hill (newspaper) - Does not use "massacre" :The Hindu - Does not use "massacre" :So this looks like mere repetition of a failed argument. [[User:Selfstudier|Selfstudier]] ([[User talk:Selfstudier|talk]]) 18:52, 28 April 2024 (UTC) ::What's changed is that reliable sources continue not to use massacre, and the additional distance from the event will allow us to better consider what title is most compliant with our policies - the previous RM did have off-wiki canvassing issues. [[User:BilledMammal|BilledMammal]] ([[User talk:BilledMammal|talk]]) 19:17, 28 April 2024 (UTC) :::No, the question was what has changed since you presented your arguments last time? [[User:AusLondonder|AusLondonder]] ([[User talk:AusLondonder|talk]]) 17:02, 29 April 2024 (UTC) *'''procedural close''' per Selfstudier this is a repetition of a failed argument. Further the term 'flour massacre' is used by RS ([https://www.washingtonpost.com/world/2024/03/01/gaza-humanitarian-catastrophe/], [https://www.un.org/unispal/document/un-experts-condemn-flour-massacre-5mar24], [https://www.france24.com/en/middle-east/20240301-flour-massacre-aid-delivery-turns-deadly-in-gaza-as-un-warns-of-inevitable-famine], [https://www.thetimes.co.uk/article/dozens-killed-by-israeli-strike-on-crowd-in-gaza-palestinian-authorities-claim-2lvkj79fp]) including the Guardian [https://www.theguardian.com/world/2024/mar/05/un-experts-condemn-israeli-massacre-of-palestinians-collecting-flour] and CNN [https://www.cnn.com/2024/04/09/middleeast/gaza-food-aid-convoy-deaths-eyewitness-intl-investigation-cmd/index.html] which are listed as not using 'massacre' at all—[[user:blindlynx|blindlynx]] 01:17, 29 April 2024 (UTC) *:Those sources aren’t using the term; they’re mentioning it. For example, CNN says {{tq|The tragedy, which has become known among Palestinians as the “Flour Massacre”}}. Even if this title wasn’t already forbidden by naming conventions, NPOV would tell us that preferencing the name preferred by a single POV in this way would be a violation. [[User:BilledMammal|BilledMammal]] ([[User talk:BilledMammal|talk]]) 01:23, 29 April 2024 (UTC) *::yeah but con is this is a case of [[WP:POVNAME]]—[[user:blindlynx|blindlynx]] 01:26, 29 April 2024 (UTC) *:::POVNAME requires that the name is used in a significant majority of English language sources - this name isn’t even used in a significant minority. [[User:BilledMammal|BilledMammal]] ([[User talk:BilledMammal|talk]]) 01:33, 29 April 2024 (UTC) *::::Sure, but consensus was to use the current name as it's '[...] descriptive of the event and recognizable'. This RM presents is just rehashing of the move review and not demonstrating that anything has changed since then. Moreover, i'm slightly concerned about your list as a few you describe as not using massacre defiantly do—[[user:blindlynx|blindlynx]] 20:43, 29 April 2024 (UTC) *::116 dead constitutes a 'massacre'. CNN [https://edition.cnn.com/2024/03/08/middleeast/gaza-flour-massacre-idf-report-intl/index.html states] that it '''became known as the 'flour massacre.''' Your proposal is not neutral but neutering.[[User:Nishidani|Nishidani]] ([[User talk:Nishidani|talk]]) 01:44, 29 April 2024 (UTC) *:::[[WP:OR|It is not our place to decide what is and isn’t a massacre]]. [[User:BilledMammal|BilledMammal]] ([[User talk:BilledMammal|talk]]) 01:48, 29 April 2024 (UTC) *::::CNN flat out says this is known as the Flour Massacre. They are reporting that this is the common name. Your OR to attempt to disprove that is immaterial. '''[[User talk:Nableezy|nableezy]]''' - 07:52, 29 April 2024 (UTC) *:::::CNN says this is known as the Flour Massacre ''in Palestine''. Further, [[WP:POVNAME]] requires use; CNN isn’t using the name. [[User:BilledMammal|BilledMammal]] ([[User talk:BilledMammal|talk]]) 07:56, 29 April 2024 (UTC) *::::::[https://edition.cnn.com/2024/03/08/middleeast/gaza-flour-massacre-idf-report-intl/index.html CNN]: {{xt|More than 100 people were killed in the incident in northern Gaza, which has become known as the “Flour Massacre,” as Israeli troops opened fire near civilians gathering around food aid trucks, triggering panic. … UN experts earlier this week condemned the incident as a “massacre,”}} BilledMammal's addendum: they only say it is known in Palestine. Nableezy: ???. And who even said massacre is POV here? '''[[User talk:Nableezy|nableezy]]''' - 07:59, 29 April 2024 (UTC) *:::::::{{ec|3}} That article is from shortly after the tragedy; a [https://www.cnn.com/2024/04/09/middleeast/gaza-food-aid-convoy-deaths-eyewitness-intl-investigation-cmd/index.html more recent one] has clarified who uses this name; {{tq| The tragedy, which has become known among Palestinians as the “Flour Massacre}}. Incidentally, you asked above for a source saying that "Flour Massacre" is not the global common name; this, as the [[exception that proves the rule]], is that source. [[User:BilledMammal|BilledMammal]] ([[User talk:BilledMammal|talk]]) 08:05, 29 April 2024 (UTC) *::::::::[https://www.cfjustice.org/un-experts-condemn-flour-massacre-call-for-immediate-ceasefire-in-gaza/#:~:text=UN%20experts%20condemned%20the%20recent,the%20southwest%20of%20Gaza%20City. UN experts] are not from Palestine, and neither is Al Jazeera, and that Palestinians *also* call it this does not mean it is not called this by others or known as the Flour Massacre as CNN reports. And no, that source does not support your conclusion, that is just silly and I expect anybody who reads your comment to reach the same conclusion. '''[[User talk:Nableezy|nableezy]]''' - 08:14, 29 April 2024 (UTC)
The discussion above is closed. Please do not modify it. Subsequent comments should be made on the appropriate discussion page. No further edits should be made to this discussion.
== Edit warring == {{ping|Utilisateur19911}} Please stop edit warring and self-revert to bring yourself into compliance with [[WP:BURDEN]] which clearly states: "The burden to demonstrate verifiability lies with the editor who '''adds or restores material'''". The burden is on you as you have restored masses of text that has nothing to do with the massacre directly and is in violation of [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Lead_section#Length LEDE] guideline. [[User:Makeandtoss|Makeandtoss]] ([[User talk:Makeandtoss|talk]]) 10:39, 8 May 2024 (UTC) :It is unclear why you keep pretending the content isn't verifiable. The two short paragraphs are supported by twenty citations, many from sources you have personally endorsed as reliable. Repeated and long-winded citation of an irrelevant policy does not make it any less irrelevant. Your assertion that the sequence of events leading up to the massacre "has nothing to do with the massacre" does not do much for your credibility. [[User:Utilisateur19911|Utilisateur19911]] ([[User talk:Utilisateur19911|talk]]) 08:01, 9 May 2024 (UTC) ::"While information must be verifiable for inclusion in an article, not all verifiable information must be included. Consensus may determine that certain information does not improve an article." ([[WP:VNOT]]) ::Exactly why is it that this information needs to be included, and in the lead? [[User:Selfstudier|Selfstudier]] ([[User talk:Selfstudier|talk]]) 10:26, 9 May 2024 (UTC) :::Furthermore the lede is a summary of the body, this may belong elsewhere, but definitely not in the lede where it obscures and overwhelms readers with everything but how the Israeli military killed hungry people and then lied about it, according to CNN. [[User:Makeandtoss|Makeandtoss]] ([[User talk:Makeandtoss|talk]]) 11:37, 9 May 2024 (UTC) ::::In other words, you don't want the facts to get in the way of your narrative. Thank you for your honesty. To mark this special moment, I vacate the field. [[User:Utilisateur19911|Utilisateur19911]] ([[User talk:Utilisateur19911|talk]]) 14:33, 9 May 2024 (UTC) :::::It's not my narrative, it's the factual narrative of CNN, a reliable source according to Wikipedia. [[User:Makeandtoss|Makeandtoss]] ([[User talk:Makeandtoss|talk]]) 14:40, 9 May 2024 (UTC) == Title formatting == If this is a definite event with a proper name, shouldn't it be "Flour Massacre", with both words capitalized? [[User:Arkadios 200|Arkadios 200]] ([[User talk:Arkadios 200|talk]]) 20:20, 4 June 2024 (UTC) :I suppose that it depends on what sources use, but lowercase is generally considered to be "default" when sources use various options. [[User:Loqiical|Loqiical]] ([[User talk:Loqiical|talk]]) 04:50, 15 June 2024 (UTC) == Minor problem with the article's titles == There's an article I've been trying to rename called "[[Al-Rasheed Street]]" into "Al-Rashid Street" for a more accurate and less confusing title as the Iraqi cultural street was named after Caliph Harun al-Rashid, and is more known as "Al-Rashid Street". But the problem is that it keeps telling me there's already an article called "Al-Rashid Street" and the only article that seems to have this title redirects to [[Flour massacre|this one]]. I would appreciate it if this redirection could be removed since I don't want to rename the article "Al-Rashid Street, Baghdad" because there's no article on Wikipedia with this title. It should be noted that this "Al-Rashid Street" is an old Iraqi cultural area unrelated to the Palestinian street which doesn't seem to have been known about generally before the massacre took place. [[User:Ayham4002|Ayham4002]] ([[User talk:Ayham4002|talk]]) 20:37, 17 July 2024 (UTC) :List it with reasons at [[Wikipedia:Redirects for discussion]]. [[User:Selfstudier|Selfstudier]] ([[User talk:Selfstudier|talk]]) 21:14, 17 July 2024 (UTC) == August 2024 == {{Edit extended-protected|answered=yes}} * '''What I think should be changed''': {{TextDiff|foreign minister Mao Ning said that|foreign ministry spokesperson Mao Ning said that}} * '''Why it should be changed''': Factual error — Mao Ning is not the Chinese foreign minister. She is a spokesperson, as mentioned in the citation and the Wikipedia page [[Mao Ning (diplomat)]]. [[User:Y. Dongchen|Y. Dongchen]] ([[User talk:Y. Dongchen|talk]]) 11:40, 30 August 2024 (UTC) :{{done}} [[User:Ilovefood123123|Ilovefood123123]] ([[User talk:Ilovefood123123|talk]]) 15:39, 30 August 2024 (UTC) == Al Jazeera Line in Intro Cites a Correspondent's Opinion == {{edit extended-protected|Flour massacre|answered=yes}} This line: "Al Jazeera said the attack was part of a broader pattern of Israeli attacks on people seeking humanitarian aid.[24]" Implies the editorial board of Al Jazeera released an official statement on such a pattern, or that there was a report documenting such a broader pattern. In fact, the linked sources cites the opinion of a correspondent. Suggest changing the line to this: "Tareq Abu Azzoum, a correspondent for Al Jazeera, suggested the attack was part of a broader pattern of Israeli attacks on people seeking humanitarian aid.[24] Alternatively, we could keep the line the same if we cite a stronger source, such as an in-depth report, or an editorial piece. [[User:Iamnotanorange|Iamnotanorange]] ([[User talk:Iamnotanorange|talk]]) 02:07, 19 January 2025 (UTC) ::{{done}} Apologies my account was forcibly migrated a few years back and I was able to make these changes on my own after logging into the correct account. Now that this sentence has been corrected based on the source, I think it's an open question whether it's worth leaving in the intro. I'll keep it for now, because it's detailing a POV. [[User:Iamnotanorange~enwiki|DuckOfOrange]] ([[User talk:Iamnotanorange~enwiki|talk]]) 09:42, 19 January 2025 (UTC) == Image caption == I don't believe the caption on the inbox is accurate. It describes the image as showing "Bodies of Palestinians near aid trucks", but I don't believe that is accurate, or rather that is misleading. I believe most of the people in the image (seen as black dots) are alive I'm not sure what the proper caption should be, probably something like "An aerial view of the massacre from an Israeli drone." [[User:IOHANNVSVERVS|IOHANNVSVERVS]] ([[User talk:IOHANNVSVERVS|talk]]) 03:00, 21 January 2025 (UTC) == Requested move 11 March 2025 ==
:''The following is a closed discussion of a [[Wikipedia:Requested moves|requested move]]. '''Please do not modify it.''' Subsequent comments should be made in a new section on the talk page. Editors desiring to contest the closing decision should consider a [[Wikipedia:move review|move review]] '''after''' discussing it on the closer's talk page. No further edits should be made to this discussion.'' The result of the move request was: '''Not moved.''' ([[Wikipedia:Requested moves/Closing instructions#Non-admin closure|non-admin closure]]) [[User:Jeffrey34555|Jeffrey34555]] ([[User talk:Jeffrey34555|talk]]) 04:01, 18 March 2025 (UTC) ---- [[:Flour massacre]] → {{no redirect|Al-Rashid aid killings}} – Beside sources with a pro-Palestinian bias, no neutral source (e.g. CNN, The Guardian, the BBC) labels the event as a massacre in its own voice. See BilledMammal's analysis for more. This is per [[WP:NCENPOV]] and deserves reexamination following [[WP:PIA5]] remedy 12. It seems from the previous RfC a mention of the term "flour massacre" is good enough, although this goes against [[WP:CONTEXTMATTERS]] (direct support, in the source's own voice, as opposed to describing another party's characterization of it). [[User:Closetside|Closetside]] ([[User talk:Closetside|talk]]) 00:09, 11 March 2025 (UTC) *'''Comment''' – This was proposed previously at [[Talk:Flour massacre#Requested move 28 April 2024]]. [[User:IOHANNVSVERVS|IOHANNVSVERVS]] ([[User talk:IOHANNVSVERVS|talk]]) 04:59, 11 March 2025 (UTC) :Also note that the discussion which resulted in the current title (which can be seen [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Talk:Flour_massacre/Archive_1#Requested_move_29_February_2024 here]) had 408 comments and the result was: "There is a consensus to move to a title with 'massacre' in it. Initial source analysis indicates that the event was not referred to as a 'massacre'. However as presented by several editors here over the course of the discussion, the word has been used as widely as it is not to refer to the event." [[User:IOHANNVSVERVS|IOHANNVSVERVS]] ([[User talk:IOHANNVSVERVS|talk]]) 05:05, 11 March 2025 (UTC) :'''Suggest this be closed''' -This should probably be closed since the user who opened this RM has stated that they won't be repsponding further (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/User_talk:Closetside#%22Retired%22?) and since their reasoning for opening the RM brings nothing new and simply ignores the result of the previous widely-participated-in RM which established consensus for the current title. [[User:IOHANNVSVERVS|IOHANNVSVERVS]] ([[User talk:IOHANNVSVERVS|talk]]) 23:55, 12 March 2025 (UTC) :'''oppose - [https://www.theguardian.com/world/2024/mar/05/un-experts-condemn-israeli-massacre-of-palestinians-collecting-flour][https://www.ohchr.org/en/press-releases/2024/03/un-experts-condemn-flour-massacre-urge-israel-end-campaign-starvation-gaza]''' and there is plenty of reference that says massacre. killing 118 is a massacre. [[User:Astropulse|Astropulse]] ([[User talk:Astropulse|talk]]) 23:58, 12 March 2025 (UTC) ::The Guardian, a neutral RS, puts the term massacre in quotes. The UN is a government org, which should be cited with attribution, not as fact, similar to all other governments and NGOs. This further proves my point about how neutral RS do not refer to this event as a massacre. The death toll is irrelevant for this determination; the question is "flour massacre" the common name to meet the standard of [[WP:NCENPOV]], and if the Guardian, a neutral source doesn't, it just isn't. [[User:Closetside|Closetside]] ([[User talk:Closetside|talk]]) 04:19, 13 March 2025 (UTC) *'''oppose''' unless something has substantially changed---which i see no evidence for---'Flour massacre' is the clear [[wp:commonname]] even if it is a [[ WP:POVNAME]]—[[user:blindlynx|blindlynx]] 13:51, 13 March 2025 (UTC) :'''Oppose''' but I believe "massacre" should be capitalized. [[WP:NCENPOV]] is the applicable policy. Flour Massacre is the [[WP:COMMONNAME]]. Death toll is irrelevant. [[User:Chess|Chess]] ([[User talk:Chess|talk]]) (please [[Help:Talk pages#Notifications|mention]] me on reply) 00:42, 18 March 2025 (UTC)
The discussion above is closed. Please do not modify it. Subsequent comments should be made on the appropriate discussion page. No further edits should be made to this discussion.
== Not an RM == @[[User:Chess|Chess]] suggested that Flour '''M'''assacre should be capitalized in the RM above. I'm not a native speaker, but this seems right to me. Is someone opposed? [[User:FortunateSons|FortunateSons]] ([[User talk:FortunateSons|talk]]) 10:54, 18 March 2025 (UTC) == Requested move 14 April 2025 ==
:''The following is a closed discussion of a [[Wikipedia:Requested moves|requested move]]. '''Please do not modify it.''' Subsequent comments should be made in a new section on the talk page. Editors desiring to contest the closing decision should consider a [[Wikipedia:move review|move review]] '''after''' discussing it on the closer's talk page. No further edits should be made to this discussion.'' The result of the move request was: '''moved.''' Community consensus reached with most supporting the nomination cause of the Wikipedia naming conventions and other users supporting. With the opposing not replying to the reply made by chess about his oppose. ([[Wikipedia:Requested moves/Closing instructions#Non-admin closure|non-admin closure]]) '''[[User:Valorrr|Valorrr]] ([[User talk:Valorrr|lets chat]])''' 02:40, 22 April 2025 (UTC) ---- [[:Flour massacre]] → {{no redirect|Flour Massacre}} – Since the term is being used as a proper noun, both words in the name should be capitalized, per [[MOS:PROPERNAME]]. [[User:Chess|Chess]] ([[User talk:Chess|talk]]) (please [[Help:Talk pages#Notifications|mention]] me on reply) 21:47, 14 April 2025 (UTC) :Also, if the move goes through, I will change the article text from "flour massacre" from "Flour Massacre". [[User:Chess|Chess]] ([[User talk:Chess|talk]]) (please [[Help:Talk pages#Notifications|mention]] me on reply) 23:31, 14 April 2025 (UTC) ::'''Support''' per nom—[[user:blindlynx|blindlynx]] 00:06, 15 April 2025 (UTC) *'''Support''' per consistency with [[Boston Massacre]], [[Bear River Massacre]], etc. Not a descriptive title like [[Bucha massacre]], but a proper name, dubbed by sources. [[Special:Contributions/Chicdat|🐔]] [[User:Chicdat|Chicdat]]  ''[[User talk:Chicdat|Bawk to me!]]'' 10:57, 15 April 2025 (UTC) :'''Support''' the name of a historical event should be treated as a proper noun and follows title case rules. [[User:Cinaroot|Cinaroot]] ([[User talk:Cinaroot|talk]]) 05:13, 17 April 2025 (UTC) :'''Strongly oppose''' This is against the English writing rules. Only 'flour' has to be with capital letter. Cf. all the other wiki page names containing the word "massacre". [[User:GreatLeader1945|GreatLeader1945]] [[User talk:GreatLeader1945|TALK]] 10:54, 17 April 2025 (UTC): ::{{tq|This is against the English writing rules. Only 'flour' has to be with capital letter.}} Can you explain the specific rules you are referencing? According to [[Wikipedia:Naming conventions (capitalization)]]: ::{{tqb|Do not capitalize the second or subsequent words in an article title, unless the title is a proper name. For multiword page titles, one should leave the second and subsequent words in lowercase unless the title phrase is a proper name that would always occur capitalized, even mid-sentence.}} ::Since "Flour Massacre" is a proper name, it should be capitalized. [[User:Chess|Chess]] ([[User talk:Chess|talk]]) (please [[Help:Talk pages#Notifications|mention]] me on reply) 18:28, 17 April 2025 (UTC) :::historical event should be treated as a proper noun [[User:Cinaroot|Cinaroot]] ([[User talk:Cinaroot|talk]]) 20:39, 17 April 2025 (UTC) :'''Strongly oppose''' per [[User:GreatLeader1945|GreatLeader1945]] [[User talk:GreatLeader1945|TALK]] [[User:Lova Falk|Lova Falk]] ([[User talk:Lova Falk|talk]]) 11:02, 17 April 2025 (UTC)
The discussion above is closed. Please do not modify it. Subsequent comments should be made on the appropriate discussion page. No further edits should be made to this discussion.
The problem with asserting that this is a [[proper name]] is that it is clearly not consistently capitalised in the source used in the article. [[User:Cinderella157|Cinderella157]] ([[User talk:Cinderella157|talk]]) 23:27, 29 May 2025 (UTC) :I asked the closer about this, but I haven't received a reply. [[User:Coxly|Coxly]] ([[User talk:Coxly|talk]]) 12:03, 14 July 2025 (UTC) == Edit request - change from passive voice == '''Original''' - The Flour Massacre (Arabic: مجزرة الطحين, romanized: majzarat aṭ-ṭaḥīn) occurred in the Gaza Strip on 29 February 2024, when at least 118 Palestinians ''were killed'' and 760 injured ''after'' Israeli forces opened fire while the Palestinians were seeking food from aid trucks on the coastal Al-Rashid Street in Gaza City. '''Suggested''' - The Flour Massacre (Arabic: مجزرة الطحين, romanized: majzarat aṭ-ṭaḥīn) occurred in the Gaza Strip on 29 February 2024, when Israeli forces opened fire on Palestinians seeking food from aid trucks on the coastal Al-Rashid Street in Gaza City, killing 118 and injuring 760. Rationale - On the [[October 7 attacks]] page, the passive tense is basically not used at all in the lead. Wikipedia should be consistent. [[Special:Contributions/222.152.247.12|222.152.247.12]] ([[User talk:222.152.247.12|talk]]) 22:24, 15 June 2025 (UTC)
